content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          327-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

September 3, 1981

Donna M. Kishi
Administrative Officer
Alameda-Contra Costa
Health Systems Agency
235 W. MacArthur Bl., Suite 700
Oakland, CA 94611

Re: Filing Obligations of Non-Voting
    Ex-Officio Board Members

Dear Ms. Kishi:

     This letter is in response to your letter requesting
a clarification of the filing responsibilities of non-voting
ex-officio board members.

     It has been the policy of this Commission to require
such ex-officio members to file Statements of Economic
Interests.

     Even though such ex-officio members cannot vote, they
may "participate in making decisions." Under the Fair
Political Practices Commission's regulations, participating
in decisions includes:

     Advises or makes recommendations to the
     decision-maker, either directly or without
     significant intervening substantive review,
     by preparing or presenting any report,
     analysis or opinion, orally or in writing,
     which requires the exercise of judgment
     on the part of the official or designated
     employee and the purpose of which is to
     influence the decision.
     2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
     18700(c)(2)(B)

Donna M. Kishi
September 3, 1981
Page Two


     Accordingly, if Mr. Nixon participates in the manner
described above, he is required to file a Statement and that
Statement will undergo the review process.

     If you have any questions, please phone me at (916)
322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Jeanette E. Turvill
                                        Documents Examiner

JET:mfa

ALAMEDA—CONTRA COSTA HEALTH SYSTEMS AGENCY
235 WEST MacARTHUR BOULEVARD Suite 700
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94611 (415) 652-5566

July 24, 1981


Jeanette Turvill
Document Examiner
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

SUBJECT: Clarence Nixon

Dear Jeanette:

Per our conversation of July 8, 1981, regarding the late
filing of Clarence Nixon's Statement of Economic Interest to the State
office. You were going to check with the State Attorney's Office if
an Ex-Officio member of a Governing Body must file a Statement with
the State. We will be interested to know the outcome of this decision
for future reference.

In any case, we expect notification of a fine for Clarence
Nixon to be withdrawn because of this unclear requirement. The HSA
sent his Statement to your office acting on good faith that it would
be on file if needed. Mr. Nixon did file his Statement with the HSA
on time, and we kept it in our files to fulfill our local requirement.
We feel it quite unfair for the State to impose this fine when this
office communicated to your office seeking clarification prior to
filing all Governing Body members Statements, if Ex Officio members
must file with the State. A State staff member was unclear of the
requirement; therefore, I commented we will send it because Mr. Nixon
does have a Statement on file with our office and if the requirement
should not be waived, Mr. Nixon would have fulfilled the State requirement.

Please let our office and Mr. Nixon know the standing of
his Statement of Economic Interest.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Sincerely,

Donna M. Kishi
Administrative Officer

DMK:vp
